column2: Shell text editing: Cmd+A select all, Shift+Arrow selection, ESC to cancel
AI error fixer: auto-suggests fixes on failure, Cmd+Shift+E to apply
Type y to launch Yazi, cd+Tab falls back to zsh-z history
Plugins already loaded by your config are no longer duplicated
Fixed: delete key after Chinese IME, terminal type for sudo+nano
Improved shell integration defaults for daily terminal editing
Shell text editing now supports Cmd+A, Shift+Arrow selection, and ESC cancel
AI error fixer can suggest and apply fixes directly in the terminal
Yazi launcher and cd+Tab fallback are now built into the default shell workflow
Shell integration compatibility is improved for SSH, Delete key behavior, and existing plugins
Starship prompt and AI shell hooks are more reliable in zsh and xterm-256color environments
First-run setup and managed shell integration refresh are more stable across environments
Fixed an early 0.6.0 zsh prompt helper issue and now regenerate the managed script correctly
Yazi now follows Kaku dark and light themes automatically, with corrected folder and file colors in both modes
Kaku now refreshes managed tmux integration automatically so Shift+wheel works in local tmux full-screen apps
Kaku now always loads its managed zsh integration when available, fixing shells that missed aliases and prompt setup
Smart Tab now only takes over Tab inside Kaku sessions by default, so other terminals keep their own completion workflow
Kaku can now follow macOS appearance automatically, so bundled dark and light themes switch with the system
Bundled defaults now respect your active custom kaku.lua path and no longer override your own font settings
Tab and pane close protection defaults are available in the bundled config and Settings TUI for safer multi-pane workflows
kaku init now detects Homebrew correctly in non-standard paths such as ~/homebrew
Bundled shell helpers keep working when Homebrew lives outside the standard macOS prefixes
Background tabs now show a bell prefix without the duplicate unread bell dot
Fish shell is now fully supported; run kaku init to configure prompt, yazi, and theme sync
Zsh integration now handles ssh alias conflicts and autosuggest widget detection automatically
zoxide replaces zsh-z for faster directory jumping; run zi for interactive fzf-powered selection
fast-syntax-highlighting replaces zsh-syntax-highlighting with richer colors and better performance
zoxide is installed via Homebrew automatically; your existing z history is preserved after import
zoxide uses frecency (frequency + recency): if z kaku goes to Kaku/kaku instead of Kaku, use z Kaku/ or zi to select
zsh-z replaces zoxide for directory jumping; your existing ~/.z history works immediately
cd+Tab falls back to zsh-z history when filesystem completion has no match
